title: acalypha australis f. lanceolata
intro: Acalypha australis f. lanceolata, also known as the Australian copperleaf, is a plant species belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant has been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and protecting against cell damage. Acalypha australis f. lanceolata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for medicinal use, with some herbalists also using it in topical applications such as creams and ointments.
